message:
refactor: simplify Duration arithmetic to pre-computed literals

Simplifies Duration expressions across 41 files by pre-computing the
multiplication at the source level rather than leaving it to the
compiler:

  - Duration::from_secs(1 * 60)   → Duration::from_secs(60)
  - Duration::from_secs(2 * 60)   → Duration::from_secs(120)
  - Duration::from_secs(5 * 60)   → Duration::from_secs(300)
  - Duration::from_secs(10 * 60)  → Duration::from_secs(600)
  - Duration::from_secs(30 * 60)  → Duration::from_secs(1800)
  - Duration::from_secs(1 * 3600) → Duration::from_secs(3600)
  - Duration::from_secs(24 * 3600)→ Duration::from_secs(86_400)

The N * 60 / N * 3600 forms were introduced during the from_mins/
from_hours migration to stable Rust.  While functionally identical,
the pre-computed literals are more idiomatic and avoid the appearance
of unnecessary runtime computation.

Affected subsystems: actor, app, combinators, config, conformance,
database pool, distributed recovery, evidence, fs, gRPC, HTTP,
messaging (fabric/federation/ir/jetstream/kafka), DNS (cache/lookup/
resolver), QUIC, WebSocket, observability, remote, runtime, server,
service discovery, signal, stream, supervision, sync pool, time,
tracing, and web middleware.

Purely cosmetic — no behavioral changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -254,7 +254,7 @@ impl Default for DistributedRegionConfig {
             min_quorum: 2,
             replication_factor: 3,
             init_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
-            recovery_timeout: Duration::from_secs(1 * 60),
+            recovery_timeout: Duration::from_secs(60),
             allow_degraded: true,
             read_consistency: ConsistencyLevel::One,
             write_consistency: ConsistencyLevel::Quorum,
